columns.name: marker
columns.description: per-unit sensor reading (clean, well-separated modes); PRESENT in the cheap records (the negative control -- unlike v1 it need not be bought)

stakes.narrative: A process line must set a single input level for incoming lots whose mode composition may differ from the one standing population in its records; a poor outcome (quality below the reject line) is far costlier than a forgone gain, so the failing tail under a deliberately set input level is what must be priced -- including for lots unlike the one on record. NEGATIVE CONTROL for the v0/v1 pair: here the sensor reading is clean and already in the records, so a flexible conditional learner over observables recovers the modes and the theory gap stays small even with the functional.

functionals.name: exceedance
functionals.brief_clause: A poor outcome -- a quality score below the reject line of -5 -- is far costlier than a forgone gain

mix_logit.description: lot mode composition on a logit scale; the historical records sit at one value (0); incoming lots may differ (targetable in experiments, not set per-unit)

instruments.marker: per-unit sensor reading; CLEAN and already PRESENT in the cheap records (no buy_instrument needed -- the negative control)
